strategic_goals:
1. Increase self-service adoption rate from 38% to 70% within six months of launch
2. Reduce average support ticket volume by 25% through improved knowledge base integration
3. Deliver a mobile-first portal experience achieving 4.5+ App Store rating
4. Consolidate three legacy portals into a single unified platform
success_criteria:
1. Self-service task completion rate exceeds 65% in first 90 days
2. Portal page load time under 1.2 seconds at P95
3. Customer satisfaction score (CSAT) improves by 15 points post-launch
4. Zero critical accessibility violations against WCAG 2.2 AA
5. Full data migration from legacy portals with less than 0.01% data loss
in_scope:
1. Customer dashboard with account overview, billing history, and usage analytics
2. Self-service workflows for plan changes, address updates, and payment methods
3. Integrated knowledge base with search and contextual help
4. Real-time notification center with email and push preferences
5. Mobile-responsive design with native-like progressive web app shell
6. Migration of 2.3M active accounts from legacy systems
out_of_scope:
1. Internal agent-facing tools — handled by separate Operations Portal project
2. Wholesale/partner portal — scheduled for Phase 2 in Q1 2027
3. Payment processing infrastructure changes — current Stripe integration remains
4. CRM replacement — Salesforce integration will use existing APIs

milestones.name: Design System and Component Library Complete
Backend API v1 Ready for Integration

risks.description: Legacy data inconsistencies could extend migration timeline — three source systems use different schema versions
risks.mitigation: Begin data profiling in Week 1; build reconciliation checks into migration pipeline

risks.description: Key frontend engineer availability may be reduced due to concurrent platform incident support rotation
risks.mitigation: Cross-train second frontend developer; negotiate reduced on-call rotation with Engineering Manager

risks.description: Third-party authentication provider contract renewal pending — could affect SSO integration timeline
risks.mitigation: Procurement to fast-track renewal; prepare fallback with built-in OAuth implementation

decisions:
1. Technology stack confirmed: Next.js frontend, Node.js BFF layer, PostgreSQL with existing Stripe integration
2. Design will follow the Meridian Design System v3 with portal-specific extensions
3. Migration will run in parallel with legacy systems active until 30 days post-launch
4. Accessibility audit will be conducted by external firm TechAccess at UAT stage
5. Go/No-Go decision gate scheduled for August 15 with steering committee
status_cadence: Weekly on Fridays — distributed via email and Confluence
standup_cadence: Daily at 9:15 AM — 15 minutes via Teams
escalation_path: Team Lead → Project Manager → Sponsor (Helena Varga) → CTO
